channel.title: china-static:crypto
channel.description: Static RSS merge for china variant (crypto)

item.title: Senate Dems urge probe into $500M crypto deal between Trumps, UAE
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: Democrats want the Senate to hold hearings on the dealings between the Trump administration, World Liberty Financial and Abu Dhabi royalty.

item.title: StarkWare introduces 'Private KYC' to address personal data breaches
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: “Identity checks today ask for your whole document when they only need one fact,” Starknet said.

item.title: CFTC sues Kentucky after state’s prediction market lawsuits
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has sued Kentucky, now the ninth state the regulator is battling in its fight over prediction markets.

item.title: Ethereum’s much-hated staking 'tax' may already be obsolete
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: Ethereum’s latest “funding crisis” has triggered a fierce debate over whether to tax staking rewards or to pursue funding from large ETH holders for new organizations like EthLabs.

item.title: $170M Ether longs liquidated as crypto market tumbles: Is ETH doomed?
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: ETH price hangs in the balance as a fresh wave of liquidations pressure the altcoin and spillover from Bitcoin’s struggles to hold $62,000 impact investor sentiment.

item.title: Starmer steps down: What Andy Burnham means for crypto in the UK
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: Burnham brings a dose of optimism to the UK crypto industry, having previously supported the blockchain industry as mayor of Manchester.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg ordered Meta staff to develop moneyless prediction market: NYT
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: The Meta CEO reportedly plans for the company to launch a prediction market independent of its other apps, allowing users to place wagers using a points system rather than money.

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

item.title: Crypto isn't problem with US economy, Louisiana senator says
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: Louisiana Senator John Kennedy largely dismissed what he called the “promotion” of cryptocurrency by the Digital Chamber CEO Cody Carbone in a Senate hearing on affordability.

item.title: Multi-year Bitcoin holder selling falls to 19-month low as halving model flags new market bottom date
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph
item.description: OG Bitcoin holders' spending dropped to a 19-month low, as market cycle indicators point to September as a potential market bottom.

item.title: Congress Schedules CLARITY Act Hearing for July 17 in New York
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine Congress Schedules CLARITY Act Hearing for July 17 in New York Congress has scheduled a July 17 hearing in New York on the CLARITY Act as momentum builds for the crypto market structure bill. This post Congress Schedules CLARITY Act Hearing for July 17 in New York first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: Craig Raw Built One of Bitcoin’s Best Wallets for Free. Apple Might Kill It by June 30.
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine Craig Raw Built One of Bitcoin’s Best Wallets for Free. Apple Might Kill It by June 30. Apple may terminate Sparrow Wallet creator Craig Raw's developer account on June 30 after he submitted a warning app to combat fake Sparrow Wallet scams. This post Craig Raw Built One of Bitcoin’s Best Wallets for Free. Apple Might Kill It by June 30. first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: Bull Bitcoin Secures MiCA License in France, Preserving Full Self-Custody and Privacy Features
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine Bull Bitcoin Secures MiCA License in France, Preserving Full Self-Custody and Privacy Features Founder Francis Pouliot announced the self-financed, three-year achievement, highlighting successful PASSI and DORA cybersecurity audits without outsourcing core infrastructure. This post Bull Bitcoin Secures MiCA License in France, Preserving Full Self-Custody and Privacy Features first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt .

item.title: European Union’s ESMA Orders Unlicensed Crypto Firms to Exit EU Market as MiCA Deadline Arrives
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine European Union’s ESMA Orders Unlicensed Crypto Firms to Exit EU Market as MiCA Deadline Arrives ESMA ordered unlicensed crypto firms to stop serving EU clients and wind down operations before MiCA's July 1, 2026 authorization deadline. This post European Union’s ESMA Orders Unlicensed Crypto Firms to Exit EU Market as MiCA Deadline Arrives first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: H100 Shareholders Approve Bitcoin Deal That Would Make It Europe’s No. 2 Listed Treasury
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine H100 Shareholders Approve Bitcoin Deal That Would Make It Europe’s No. 2 Listed Treasury H100 shareholders approved a deal to acquire two Norwegian bitcoin treasury firms, a move that would boost the company's holdings to 3,500 BTC. This post H100 Shareholders Approve Bitcoin Deal That Would Make It Europe’s No. 2 Listed Treasury first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: US Senate Passes Housing Bill With Four-Year Fed CBDC Ban
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine US Senate Passes Housing Bill With Four-Year Fed CBDC Ban The U.S. Senate has passed a housing bill that includes a ban on a Federal Reserve-issued CBDC through 2030. This post US Senate Passes Housing Bill With Four-Year Fed CBDC Ban first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: Nakamoto Inc. (NAKA) Closes Last Healthcare Clinic, Completes Full Pivot to Bitcoin
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine Nakamoto Inc. (NAKA) Closes Last Healthcare Clinic, Completes Full Pivot to Bitcoin Nakamoto has completed its transformation into a pure-play Bitcoin company, shutting its final healthcare clinic and ending all remaining ties to its healthcare business. This post Nakamoto Inc. (NAKA) Closes Last Healthcare Clinic, Completes Full Pivot to Bitcoin first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: Bitcoin Suisse Secures MiCAR License, Launches European Expansion from Liechtenstein
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Suisse Secures MiCAR License, Launches European Expansion from Liechtenstein Bitcoin Suisse secured a MiCAR license in Liechtenstein, enabling the crypto financial services firm to expand regulated operations across the European Union under a unified regulatory framework. This post Bitcoin Suisse Secures MiCAR License, Launches European Expansion from Liechtenstein first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: Trump Signs Quantum Computing Orders — What Does This Mean For Bitcoin?
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine Trump Signs Quantum Computing Orders — What Does This Mean For Bitcoin? Trump’s recent executive orders will accelerate the U.S. quantum computing and post-quantum encryption timelines. This post Trump Signs Quantum Computing Orders — What Does This Mean For Bitcoin? first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Deal, Launches Institutional Crypto Division
item.dc:creator: Bitcoin Magazine
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Deal, Launches Institutional Crypto Division Franklin Templeton has completed its acquisition of 250 Digital. This post Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Deal, Launches Institutional Crypto Division first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: Meet Qwable: The Free Local Model That Thinks Like Claude Fable
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: Someone fine-tuned Fable 5's reasoning style into a local Qwen model. Then someone else removed its conscience.

item.title: Trump's Quantum Push Wins Praise, But Experts Warn Bitcoin Isn't Ready
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: Industry leaders welcomed President Trump's quantum executive orders, while researchers said the accelerated timelines reflect growing urgency around post-quantum security and cryptocurrency infrastructure.

item.title: Crypto’s Clarity Act Has a New Enemy: Catholic Leaders
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: A group of 82 Catholic leaders warned a key provision of the crypto bill, protecting software developers from prosecution, could enable human trafficking.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Wants a Prediction Market Too: NYT
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: The initiative follows bets on stablecoins and the metaverse.

item.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Cyber AI Beats Anthropic's Banned Mythos Model—And Nobody's Shutting It Down
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: GPT-5.5-Cyber tops the CyberGym leaderboard as Anthropic's best models sit offline under a Trump administration export ban.

item.title: AI Agent Triggers Nuclear Strike After Getting Outmaneuvered in Civilization VI
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: A new benchmark designed to test strategic reasoning found an AI-controlled empire spent 50 turns developing nuclear weapons to stop a rival's cultural victory—only to lose the game anyway.

item.title: A New OpenClaw Competitor: ToqanClaw Promises Privacy in AI Agent Race
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: Prosus launched ToqanClaw, a no-code AI platform positioned as an European alternative to AI agents like OpenClaw.

item.title: Bitcoin Tests Two-Week Low at $62K as Tech Stocks Waver on Wall Street
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: Investors are digesting a hawkish Fed, with risk-off sentiment driving a sell-off in AI and crypto

item.title: Ethereum Foundation Cuts 20% of Workforce in 'Leaner' Reorganization
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: The non-profit is cutting 54 jobs as it reorganizes into focused "clusters," following a succession of leadership departures.

item.title: Thailand Expands Crypto Mining Probe Into $300M Chinese Laundering Network
item.dc:creator: Decrypt
item.description: Thailand's DSI says a Chinese "grey capital" network used illegal crypto mining and cash mules to launder more than $300 million a year.
